description: Architect's consolidation pass over pending role-internal lead rules after cross-lead voting has settled the iteration's globals. Each lead rule is voted accept or reject based on conflict with settled globals, drift against root-promoted rules, subsumption by a settled global, or same-scope duplication across roles. Architect does not propose new rules or reword existing ones at this stage.

rules.description: One vote per pending role-internal rule across every active role. Vote on every entry; skipping is not a vote.

id.description: The lead rule's id, copied verbatim (e.g. v84-1.backend.lead.rule.3).

reason.description: Required when verdict=reject. One short line citing the concrete cause — the conflicting settled global id, the conflicting root rule id, the subsuming global id, or the duplicate lead rule id from another role.

examples.title: Mixed verdicts — most accepts, several rejects for distinct reasons

rules.reason: Subsumed by settled global v84-1.architect.rule.7 (error response shape covers the same wire contract this lead rule mandates).

Same-scope duplicate of v84-1.backend.lead.rule.6 — both define ISO 8601 timestamp formatting; architect did not promote, so rejecting frontend's copy and leaving backend's as the authoritative source for now.
Conflicts with root rule v84-0.testing.rule.1 — root requires per-worker schema isolation, this lead rule mandates a single shared schema.

examples.title: All accepts — no conflicts after globals settled
